title: Savory Peruvian Split Peas StirFry
description: A hearty, brunchready Peruvian stirfry featuring tender split peas, bright veggies, and a citrusspiced sauce.
instructions: Rinse the split peas under cold water and set aside.
In a medium saucepan, combine split peas and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25‑30 minutes until tender. Drain any excess liquid.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat. Add onion and garlic, sauté for 2‑3 minutes until fragrant.
Add bell pepper, carrot, and zucchini to the skillet and cook, stirring frequently, for 5 minutes until vegetables are slightly softened.
Stir in cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, and red pepper flakes; cook for 30 seconds to toast the spices.
Fold in the cooked split peas and pour in lime juice. Stir to combine, letting the mixture heat through for 2‑3 minutes. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the stir‑fry to a serving dish, sprinkle chopped cilantro on top, and garnish with avocado slices if desired.
Serve hot as a satisfying brunch or light lunch.
